signal_title: European Commission's Scrutiny of Platforms' Safeguards for Minors
risk_rationale: The European Commission's scrutiny on age verification systems poses a material compliance gap for TikTok's current age assurance posture, which relies on machine learning and safety reviews. This regulatory focus could necessitate immediate enhancements to ensure minors are adequately protected from harmful content.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: TikTok states it uses machine learning and safety review to identify likely under-13 users and remove/limit access.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Investigative actions on age verification systems with a focus on preventing minors from accessing harmful content.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The current age verification system may not meet the heightened scrutiny and requirements for preventing minors from accessing harmful content as per the European Commission's investigative actions.

remediations.title: Enhance Age Verification Systems
remediations.description: Implement more robust age verification mechanisms, potentially incorporating third-party verification services, to ensure compliance with EU regulatory expectations on minor protection.
acceptance_criteria: Integration of third-party age verification services
Demonstrable reduction in minors accessing harmful content
Compliance with EU regulatory guidance on age verification

business_impact: Failure to comply with EU regulatory guidance on minor protection could result in significant fines and restrictions on platform operations within the EU market.
recommended_actions: Conduct a comprehensive review of current age verification processes.
Engage with third-party age verification providers to explore integration options.
Develop a roadmap for implementing enhanced age verification systems in the EU market.
